item.title: Exxon fracks its blue state exit
item.description: The oil major’s investors overwhelmingly voted in favour of its proposal to move its corporate domicile to Texas

item.title: Chip stocks race towards biggest gains since dotcom era on AI demand
item.description: Philadelphia Semiconductor Index rides Big Tech’s data centre spending spree to 75% gains in 2026

item.title: UK would block Indian billionaire raising BT stake
item.description: Officials cite need to maintain sovereign control over critical national infrastructure

item.title: European tank maker vows to guard against political meddling as it readies IPO
item.description: Avoiding power struggles between France and Germany is seen to be key to the success of the group

item.title: Kirkland & Ellis to spend $500mn building its own AI technology
item.description: World’s highest-grossing law firm plans to put the ‘collective intelligence’ of its lawyers into a tech platform

item.title: How to close AI’s accountability loophole
item.description: Governance of new technologies must be determined by elected officials rather than fastest moving companies

item.title: SpaceX and the Zuckerberg discount
item.description: A founder-entrenching shareholder structure can do a great deal of harm

item.title: Superdrug IPO would put lipstick on a drab UK retail sector
item.description: Superdrug grew almost twice as fast as Boots over past four years

item.title: Ukraine is turning the tables
item.description: The country’s war effort was at a nadir. But mass drone production has come to the rescue

item.title: Aircraft owners leasing out engines rather than whole planes
item.description: Industry-wide shortage has sent their values soaring

item.title: What are my rights when buying a house with my unmarried partner?
item.description: We will both pay towards the mortgage, but it won’t be a 50/50 split

item.title: Europe’s Climate Leaders 2026: interactive listing
item.description: Annual list highlights companies making progress on cutting carbon emissions

item.title: China’s farming giant Syngenta says it has no ‘nefarious’ intentions in the US
item.description: State-owned company’s CEO calls to exclude farming and agriculture from tensions between two superpowers

item.title: No, now is not the time for UK rent controls
item.description: Three think-tanks are promoting curbs to rental growth — but that horse has already bolted

item.title: The parents seeking out quirky preloved children’s clothes
item.description: Generation A are being dressed in retro tees and lace-edged blouses, sourced on Etsy, TikTok and Instagram

item.title: How Ukraine’s drones turned the tables
item.description: The country’s war effort was at a nadir. But mass drone production has come to the rescue

item.title: Nvidia chief Jensen Huang to join board at prestigious Beijing university
item.description: Chipmaker boss’s move to join Tim Cook-chaired board underlines push to maintain ties with China

item.title: Google engineer charged with insider trading on Polymarket
item.description: Employee allegedly used account name ‘AlphaRaccoon’ and amassed more than $1mn, charges say

item.title: ‘China-maxxing’ helps spur an inbound tourism boom
item.description: Beijing has found an economic win by drawing more international visitors to the country

item.title: FirstFT: World leaders flock to China to see Xi Jinping
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Samsung workers set for $400,000 bonus, and Byju’s founder sentenced to jail in Singapore

item.title: US-backed rare earth rivals clash over alleged technology theft
item.description: Legal battle erupts between mining companies supported by Trump administration to break dependence on China

item.title: Uber adds to its Delivery Hero stake at €12bn valuation
item.description: Share purchase from Aspex Management steps up US ride-hailing group’s pursuit of German food delivery company

item.title: Uber bid for Delivery Hero would be an odd route to superapp status
item.description: Food delivery group’s taxed operating profit this year is only about 2.4 per cent of its enterprise value

item.title: For ailing Lululemon, going private might not be a stretch
item.description: The company has essentially no net debt and still generated $1bn in free cash flow last year

item.title: Preventing a ‘Chernobyl moment’ in AI
item.description: A White House order on testing frontier models would be a significant first step

item.title: EU pushes for ‘tech sovereignty’ to cut reliance on US
item.description: Draft strategy marks shift from regulating Big Tech to favouring European services

item.title: ExxonMobil wins decisive backing to move corporate domicile to Texas
item.description: Oil major to relocate incorporation from New Jersey as it seeks to parry investor activism

item.title: Robinhood to let investors use AI chatbots for share trading
item.description: US brokerage joins ‘arms race’ to give retail investors new tools

item.title: The future of consulting
item.description: Plus, why so many young people are leaving academia and the unprecedented costs of being a grandparent

item.title: Fifa subpoenaed over World Cup ticket practices in two US states
item.description: New York joins with New Jersey in probe of ‘sky-high prices’ just weeks before tournament kicks off
